meaning: adj.装聪明样的,自以为聪明的,聪明的,伶俐的,有见识的
examples: As you say, not a sapient choice, perhaps, but brave, sure.
If you follow her sapient advice, you will be sure to succeed.
Contrary to popular belief, most computers are neither sapient nor hostile.
Contrast that with rival Sapient, whose co-chiefs, Jerry Greenberg and Stuart Moore, share 42% of the equity.
